description: Chapter image manifest for BA: Law of the Firm / The Neon Edge

images.visual_summary: Zeeva's journey begins against the backdrop of New Boston 2077. The scene establishes the fundamental question of why law matters to business formation, introducing the Four Problems framework that will guide the entire narrative.
images.detected_setting: New Boston 2077 — establishing shot of the cyberpunk city with Zeeva entering the narrative
images.fit_assessment: Image16 directly depicts the 'Why Law' introductory themes, making it the natural hero for Chapter 1. The overview composition matches the chapter's survey of all Four Problems.
reasons_for_fit: Introductory visual tone matches Chapter 1 scope
Establishes Zeeva as protagonist at the start of the firm lifecycle
Four Problems thematic breadth aligns with the chapter's survey purpose
images.focal_region_notes: Zeeva centered or slightly off-center; city backdrop provides depth
images.mobile_crop_notes: Crop to center on Zeeva; city skyline can bleed off edges without losing meaning
images.alt_text: Zeeva stands at the threshold of New Boston 2077, the cyberpunk skyline stretching behind her as she begins her journey through the law of business associations.
images.short_caption: Where law meets the firm — Zeeva enters New Boston 2077.
images.internal_editorial_note: Image16 numbered last but narratively first. Confirm intro framing works as a chapter-opener rather than epilogue.
images.crop_strategy_desktop: Full-width banner; preserve skyline and Zeeva's figure in lower-third or center
images.crop_strategy_mobile: Center crop on Zeeva; allow skyline to be partially occluded

images.visual_summary: Zeeva navigates agency relationships in a cyberpunk office environment. Visible indicators of control, authority lines, and principal-agent dynamics set the scene for the Attribution problem.
images.detected_setting: Cyberpunk professional office — holographic control panels and authority-line displays
images.fit_assessment: Image01 directly depicts agency relationships and the control dynamics central to Chapter 2. The professional cyberpunk setting aligns with the Attribution problem focus.
reasons_for_fit: Agency/control visual cues map directly to Chapter 2 doctrine
Cyberpunk office setting is consistent with the Neon Edge aesthetic
Attribution problem is front and center
images.focal_region_notes: Zeeva and agency-relationship indicators should remain visible; authority lines as secondary focal point
images.mobile_crop_notes: Tighten to Zeeva and primary interlocutor; agency indicators in mid-ground
images.alt_text: Zeeva discusses agency relationships in a cyberpunk office, holographic authority lines and control indicators glowing around her.
images.short_caption: Who acts for whom — agency law in the neon glow.
images.internal_editorial_note: Verify agency/control indicators are legible at card size. May need inset detail crop for mobile.
images.crop_strategy_desktop: Full-width banner preserving interpersonal dynamics and holographic overlays
images.crop_strategy_mobile: Center on Zeeva and closest interlocutor; holographic detail secondary

images.visual_summary: A partnership formation scene in New Boston. Two or more individuals discuss business terms with partnership agreement documents visible, capturing the handshake-to-contract essence of partnership law.
images.detected_setting: New Boston meeting space — partnership documents and agreement terms displayed holographically
images.fit_assessment: Image02 shows partnership formation and negotiation, mapping cleanly to Chapter 3's focus on the Risk problem through unlimited liability and partner dynamics.
reasons_for_fit: Partnership agreement documents visible in scene
Multi-party negotiation setting matches partnership formation doctrine
Risk/unlimited-liability themes are visually implied
images.focal_region_notes: Partners at table with documents between them; agreement text as secondary focal element
images.mobile_crop_notes: Crop to the two primary figures and the document between them
images.alt_text: Business partners negotiate terms around a table in New Boston, holographic partnership agreements floating between them.
images.short_caption: Handshake to contract — partnership forms in the neon city.
images.internal_editorial_note: Strong pairing with Chapter 3. Confirm partnership docs are legible at reduced sizes.
images.crop_strategy_desktop: Wide shot preserving all parties and the agreement documents
images.crop_strategy_mobile: Center on the two primary partners and the key document

images.visual_summary: A futuristic corporate environment where technology and entity shielding concepts converge. The scene captures incorporation as a technological act — partitioning assets and liabilities through the corporate form.
images.detected_setting: High-tech corporate campus or boardroom in New Boston — digital entity-shielding barriers visible
images.fit_assessment: Image03 visualizes entity shielding and corporate technology themes, directly serving Chapter 4's exploration of the Partitioning problem through the corporate form.
reasons_for_fit: Entity shielding concepts are visually represented
Corporate/technology fusion matches Chapter 4 themes
Partitioning problem is the dominant doctrinal signal
images.focal_region_notes: Corporate structure and shielding visualization in center; Zeeva as anchor figure
images.mobile_crop_notes: Crop to the entity-shielding barrier and Zeeva; corporate backdrop secondary
images.alt_text: A futuristic corporate campus in New Boston with glowing entity-shielding barriers visualizing the separation between firm and personal assets.
images.short_caption: The corporate shield — partitioning assets in the digital age.
images.internal_editorial_note: Entity shielding visualization is the key doctrinal signal. Ensure it reads clearly at all sizes.
images.crop_strategy_desktop: Full-width showing corporate environment and shielding barriers
images.crop_strategy_mobile: Focus on the shielding visualization; corporate campus as background

images.visual_summary: An LLC formation scene emphasizing flexibility and contractual freedom. Operating agreement elements are visible as Zeeva explores the most adaptable business entity form in New Boston's legal landscape.
images.detected_setting: Adaptable workspace in New Boston — modular furniture and holographic operating-agreement clauses
images.fit_assessment: Image04 captures LLC formation and the contractual freedom at the heart of Chapter 5. The flexible, modular aesthetic matches the Governance problem focus on LLC adaptability.
reasons_for_fit: Operating agreement elements are visible in the scene
Flexible/modular visual design mirrors LLC contractual freedom
Governance problem through LLC structure is central
images.focal_region_notes: Operating agreement clauses and Zeeva; modular workspace as framing device
images.mobile_crop_notes: Center on Zeeva and the operating agreement display; modular elements secondary
images.alt_text: Zeeva reviews holographic operating agreement clauses in a modular New Boston workspace, embodying the LLC's contractual flexibility.
images.short_caption: Contractual freedom — the LLC takes shape.
images.internal_editorial_note: Image00 also has operating-agreement elements but fits ch8 better. Image04 is the clean LLC pick.
images.crop_strategy_desktop: Wide banner preserving workspace modularity and agreement clauses
images.crop_strategy_mobile: Tight crop on Zeeva and the primary agreement display

images.visual_summary: A community-focused setting where nonprofit and social enterprise themes take center stage. The nondistribution constraint is implied through imagery of collective benefit rather than private profit extraction.
images.detected_setting: Community hub or social enterprise space in New Boston — collective benefit iconography and shared resources
images.fit_assessment: Image05 aligns with Chapter 6's exploration of nonprofits and the Governance problem through the nondistribution constraint. The community-focused aesthetic is a strong match.
reasons_for_fit: Community-focused visual design matches nonprofit doctrine
Nondistribution constraint implied through collective-benefit imagery
Governance problem is the doctrinal anchor
images.focal_region_notes: Community group and shared-resource imagery; Zeeva integrated among the group
images.mobile_crop_notes: Crop to Zeeva and the nearest community members; shared-resource elements in mid-ground
images.alt_text: Zeeva engages with community members in a New Boston social enterprise space, collective benefit themes glowing around them.
images.short_caption: Beyond profit — the nonprofit constraint in neon light.
images.internal_editorial_note: Verify communal imagery doesn't read as generic crowd scene at card size.
images.crop_strategy_desktop: Full-width preserving the community group and shared-resource context
images.crop_strategy_mobile: Center on Zeeva and 2-3 community members

images.visual_summary: A decentralized governance scene rich with blockchain and digital elements. Code-as-law themes dominate as the DAO form challenges traditional Attribution assumptions — who is the principal when the code is the agent?
images.detected_setting: Digital/virtual DAO governance space — blockchain node network and smart-contract code projections
images.fit_assessment: Image06 is a natural fit for Chapter 7's DAO focus. The blockchain/code-as-law imagery directly serves the Attribution problem in the decentralized context.
reasons_for_fit: Blockchain and smart-contract visuals are prominent
Code-as-law theme maps directly to Chapter 7 doctrine
Attribution problem in decentralized governance is visually implied
images.focal_region_notes: Blockchain node network and Zeeva; smart-contract code as background texture
images.mobile_crop_notes: Center on Zeeva with blockchain nodes radiating outward; code projections as ambient detail
images.alt_text: Zeeva stands within a decentralized blockchain network in New Boston, smart-contract code projections illuminating the DAO governance space.
images.internal_editorial_note: DAO imagery should feel distinct from the corporate tech scene in ch4. Verify visual differentiation.
images.crop_strategy_desktop: Full-width showcasing the decentralized network radiating from center
images.crop_strategy_mobile: Center on Zeeva and nearest blockchain nodes; network extends off-screen

images.visual_summary: Zeeva (silver-haired, gray professional dress) and Sammy (older man, gray suit) sit at a futuristic glass conference table with holographic displays showing 'Entity Structure & Decision Pathways', 'Authority Lines', 'Risk/Loss Sharing', 'Limited Liability Allocation', and 'Draft Operating Agreement (Rev. 1)'. The New Boston skyline with cranes, monorail, and drones fills the background through floor-to-ceiling windows.
images.detected_setting: Modern high-rise office in New Boston 2077 — floor-to-ceiling windows, holographic decision-pathway displays, New Boston skyline with cranes and monorail
images.fit_assessment: Image00 is the strongest Chapter 8 candidate. Its holographic displays explicitly reference all Four Problems, and the Zeeva-Sammy pairing signals the mentorship dynamic central to entity selection deliberation.
reasons_for_fit: Holographic labels directly name entity-selection concepts (Authority Lines, Risk/Loss Sharing, etc.)
All Four Problems are visually represented in a single image
Zeeva and Sammy's deliberative posture matches the synthesis chapter
Operating agreement draft connects to the practical entity-selection decision
images.focal_region_notes: Zeeva and Sammy at the conference table as primary focal point; holographic displays as secondary; skyline as depth layer
images.mobile_crop_notes: Crop to Zeeva and Sammy at the table with 1-2 holographic labels visible; skyline can be occluded
images.alt_text: Zeeva and Sammy deliberate at a glass conference table surrounded by holographic displays of entity structures, authority lines, and risk-sharing frameworks, with the New Boston skyline beyond.
images.short_caption: All Four Problems on the table — choosing the right entity.
images.internal_editorial_note: Primary hero for ch8. Image07 serves as alternate. Image00 also exists at repo root (BA-LotF_Image00.png) — same asset, different location. Also has ch5/LLC operating-agreement overlap, but the all-Four-Problems scope makes ch8 the better fit.
images.crop_strategy_desktop: Full-width banner; preserve both figures, holographic displays, and skyline depth
images.crop_strategy_mobile: Center on Zeeva and Sammy; keep at least two holographic labels visible; skyline as soft bokeh

images.visual_summary: An alternate entity-selection scene showing multiple business forms being evaluated side by side. The decision-making process is visualized through comparison frameworks as the characters weigh competing entity structures.
images.detected_setting: Evaluation or strategy room in New Boston — multiple entity-form holographic cards or comparison panels
images.fit_assessment: Image07 is a viable alternate for Chapter 8 but lacks the explicit holographic labels of Image00. Better suited as a secondary asset — useful for card views, inset diagrams, or A/B layout testing.
reasons_for_fit: Entity-comparison visual concept aligns with Chapter 8 themes
Decision-making process is the central visual action
All Four Problems are implicitly addressed through form comparison
reasons_for_mismatch: Less explicit doctrinal labeling than Image00
Missing the Zeeva-Sammy mentorship dynamic that anchors Image00
Compositionally less distinctive at card/thumbnail size
images.focal_region_notes: Entity comparison panels as primary focal point; Zeeva reviewing them
images.mobile_crop_notes: Crop to 2-3 entity comparison panels and Zeeva
images.alt_text: Multiple business entity forms displayed as holographic comparison cards while Zeeva evaluates the options in a New Boston strategy room.
images.short_caption: Weighing the forms — entity selection, side by side.
images.internal_editorial_note: Alternate to Image00 for ch8. Useful for card/inset or if Image00 is reassigned. Competes directly with Image00 — Image00 wins on doctrinal specificity.
images.crop_strategy_mobile: Focus on 2-3 comparison panels; Zeeva as anchor

images.visual_summary: A governance-focused scene centered on fiduciary duties — loyalty, care, and good faith. A boardroom or judicial setting evokes the weight of trust obligations, with visual tension between duty and self-interest.
images.detected_setting: Boardroom or quasi-judicial governance chamber in New Boston — trust iconography and loyalty/care duality
images.fit_assessment: Image08 (note: hyphen in filename) directly depicts fiduciary duty themes for Chapter 9. The loyalty/care tension is visually rendered in the boardroom setting.
reasons_for_fit: Fiduciary duty imagery — trust, loyalty, care — is central
Boardroom/governance setting matches Chapter 9 doctrine
Governance problem is the dominant signal
images.focal_region_notes: Board members and Zeeva around a governance table; trust/loyalty motifs in the environment
images.mobile_crop_notes: Center on Zeeva and the fiduciary tension; boardroom as ambient context
images.alt_text: Zeeva faces a boardroom in New Boston where fiduciary duties of loyalty and care are weighed, trust iconography glowing in the chamber.
images.short_caption: Loyalty, care, good faith — the fiduciary tightrope.
images.internal_editorial_note: Note the filename uses a hyphen (BA-LotF-Image08) instead of underscore. Normalize during migration.
images.crop_strategy_desktop: Full-width boardroom scene preserving the governance-table dynamics
images.crop_strategy_mobile: Tight crop on Zeeva and the nearest board member; trust motifs as ambient glow

images.visual_summary: A venture capital and private funding scene where investor negotiations unfold behind closed doors. The tension between capital need and control retention defines the visual — staying private means staying in control.
images.detected_setting: Private meeting room or VC office in New Boston — closed doors, confidential displays, term-sheet projections
images.fit_assessment: Image09 captures the private-funding dynamics of Chapter 10. The VC negotiation setting and Risk problem focus on capital-vs-control tension are well matched.
reasons_for_fit: VC/private funding negotiation setting aligns with Chapter 10
Term-sheet and investor dynamics are visually present
Risk problem through venture capital is the doctrinal anchor
images.focal_region_notes: Zeeva and investors across a negotiation table; term-sheet display as secondary focal point
images.mobile_crop_notes: Center on Zeeva and the primary investor; term sheet visible but secondary
images.alt_text: Zeeva negotiates with venture capitalists in a private New Boston meeting room, holographic term sheets illuminating the stakes of staying private.
images.short_caption: Capital without disclosure — the private path.
images.internal_editorial_note: Strong ch10 fit. Verify VC/investor visual cues are distinct from ch11 (going public).
images.crop_strategy_desktop: Full-width preserving negotiation dynamics and term-sheet displays
images.crop_strategy_mobile: Tight crop on Zeeva and lead investor; term sheet as ambient detail

images.visual_summary: An IPO and public offering scene capturing the moment a firm crosses from private to public. Stock exchange elements, disclosure frameworks, and the Partitioning problem through public-market accountability define the image.
images.detected_setting: New Boston stock exchange or public-offering venue — ticker displays, IPO ceremony elements, disclosure projections
images.fit_assessment: Image10 captures the IPO threshold central to Chapter 11. The Partitioning problem through public-market disclosure is the key doctrinal signal. Smaller file size (2.3MB) — verify resolution is sufficient for hero use.
reasons_for_fit: Stock exchange and IPO elements directly match Chapter 11
Disclosure/public-offering themes are visually prominent
Partitioning problem through public accountability is central
reasons_for_mismatch: Smaller file size (2.3MB vs 7+ MB peers) — may have lower resolution
images.focal_region_notes: IPO moment and stock exchange elements as primary; Zeeva as anchor figure
images.mobile_crop_notes: Center on the IPO ceremony or ticker display; Zeeva visible but scene-setting is primary
images.alt_text: The New Boston stock exchange comes alive as Zeeva's firm goes public, ticker displays and disclosure projections marking the IPO threshold.
images.short_caption: Crossing the threshold — the IPO in neon light.
images.internal_editorial_note: Smaller file (2.3MB). Verify resolution at hero size. If insufficient, consider using at card/tile size only and sourcing a higher-res hero.
images.crop_strategy_desktop: Full-width featuring the stock exchange and IPO ceremony
images.crop_strategy_mobile: Center on the ticker/IPO moment; Zeeva as contextual anchor

images.visual_summary: A shareholder voting and corporate democracy scene. Proxy fights, contested elections, and the Governance problem through shareholder power define the image — who controls the corporation when shareholders mobilize?
images.detected_setting: Shareholder meeting hall or proxy-voting arena in New Boston — holographic ballot displays and shareholder tally boards
images.fit_assessment: Image11 directly depicts shareholder franchise themes for Chapter 12. The proxy-fight and corporate-democracy imagery maps cleanly to the Governance problem.
reasons_for_fit: Shareholder voting and proxy-fight elements are visually prominent
Corporate democracy setting matches Chapter 12 doctrine
Governance problem through shareholder mobilization is central
images.focal_region_notes: Voting mechanisms and shareholder assembly as primary; Zeeva navigating the scene
images.mobile_crop_notes: Crop to the voting display and Zeeva; shareholder crowd as ambient
images.alt_text: Shareholders mobilize in a New Boston corporate meeting hall, holographic ballots and proxy tallies illuminating the contest for corporate control.
images.short_caption: Corporate democracy — shareholders take the vote.
images.internal_editorial_note: Strong ch12 fit. Verify proxy/voting imagery is distinct from general boardroom scenes.
images.crop_strategy_desktop: Full-width showing the shareholder assembly and voting displays
images.crop_strategy_mobile: Center on the voting display; shareholder assembly as background

images.visual_summary: A merger and acquisition negotiation scene where corporate entities collide or combine. The Governance problem manifests through takeover dynamics — hostile bids, defensive measures, and the contest for corporate control at the highest stakes.
images.detected_setting: High-stakes negotiation room in New Boston — dual corporate logos, merger-term projections, takeover defense schematics
images.fit_assessment: Image12 captures the M&A dynamics of Chapter 13. The takeover negotiation imagery and Governance problem focus on control contests are a strong match.
reasons_for_fit: M&A negotiation and merger imagery are central
Takeover dynamics visually imply Governance problem stakes
Corporate combination themes match Chapter 13 doctrine
images.focal_region_notes: Merger negotiation table and corporate-entity combination imagery; Zeeva at the center
images.mobile_crop_notes: Center on the merger negotiation; dual corporate logos or entities visible
images.alt_text: Corporate entities converge in a high-stakes New Boston negotiation room as Zeeva navigates the merger and acquisition battlefield.
images.short_caption: When firms collide — M&A in the neon city.
images.internal_editorial_note: Verify M&A imagery is visually distinct from ch10 (staying private) and ch12 (shareholder franchise).
images.crop_strategy_desktop: Full-width capturing the dual-entity merger dynamics
images.crop_strategy_mobile: Center on the merger action; negotiation table as anchor

images.visual_summary: Corporate boundaries dissolve as the veil-piercing doctrine is visualized. The Partitioning problem fails — alter ego, commingling, and undercapitalization breach the entity shield, exposing personal assets behind the corporate form.
images.detected_setting: Corporate/legal threshold space in New Boston — entity barriers cracking or dissolving, personal and corporate assets becoming visible through the breach
images.fit_assessment: Image13 powerfully depicts the veil-piercing doctrine for Chapter 14. The dissolving corporate boundaries are the perfect visual metaphor for the Partitioning problem's failure mode.
reasons_for_fit: Corporate boundaries dissolving is the exact veil-piercing visual metaphor
Alter ego and commingling themes are implied through the breach
Partitioning problem failure is the doctrinal core
images.focal_region_notes: Dissolving corporate barrier as primary; exposed assets behind it; Zeeva witnessing the breach
images.mobile_crop_notes: Center on the dissolving barrier; Zeeva and exposed assets visible
images.alt_text: Corporate boundaries crack and dissolve in New Boston, exposing personal assets behind the corporate veil as the entity shield fails.
images.short_caption: When the shield breaks — piercing the corporate veil.
images.internal_editorial_note: One of the most visually dramatic images. Ensure the dissolution effect reads clearly at all sizes.
images.crop_strategy_desktop: Full-width featuring the dissolving barrier and asset exposure
images.crop_strategy_mobile: Center on the breach point; Zeeva and dissolving barrier as dual focus

images.visual_summary: Creditor-debtor dynamics and financial structure elements define this scene. The Risk problem is explored through solvency, capital stacking, and the tension between equity holders and creditors in the firm's financial architecture.
images.detected_setting: Financial architecture space in New Boston — layered capital-structure displays, creditor/equity tiers, solvency indicators
images.fit_assessment: Image14 captures capital structure themes for Chapter 15. The creditor-debtor and solvency imagery maps to the Risk problem. Smaller file (2.7MB) — verify resolution for hero use.
reasons_for_fit: Capital-stack and financial-structure imagery are central
Creditor-debtor dynamics match Chapter 15 doctrine
Risk problem through solvency is the doctrinal anchor
reasons_for_mismatch: Smaller file size (2.7MB vs 7+ MB peers) — may have lower resolution
images.focal_region_notes: Capital-structure layers and creditor/equity tiers as primary; Zeeva navigating the structure
images.mobile_crop_notes: Center on the capital stack; Zeeva as contextual anchor
images.alt_text: Layered capital-structure tiers glow in a New Boston financial space, creditor and equity positions stacked as Zeeva examines the firm's financial architecture.
images.short_caption: Debt, equity, solvency — the capital stack revealed.
images.internal_editorial_note: Smaller file (2.7MB). Verify resolution at hero size. If insufficient, may need upscaling or card-only use.
images.crop_strategy_desktop: Full-width featuring the layered capital structure
images.crop_strategy_mobile: Center on the most prominent capital-stack layers; Zeeva secondary

images.visual_summary: A synthesis and capstone scene where the full lifecycle is reviewed. All Four Problems converge as Zeeva reflects on the complete journey — from formation to conclusion, the firm's life rendered in neon retrospect.
images.detected_setting: Panoramic reflection space in New Boston — the full firm lifecycle visualized as a timeline or tapestry, Four Problems as recurring threads
images.fit_assessment: Image15 serves as the capstone image for Chapter 16. The synthesis/retrospective composition wraps all Four Problems into a concluding visual.
reasons_for_fit: Synthesis and retrospective framing match Chapter 16's capstone role
All Four Problems are implied through lifecycle review
Culminating mood is appropriate for the final chapter
images.focal_region_notes: Zeeva in a reflective posture with the lifecycle panorama behind her; Four Problems as recurring visual motifs
images.mobile_crop_notes: Center on Zeeva; lifecycle panorama as soft background
images.alt_text: Zeeva reflects on the full lifecycle of the firm in a panoramic New Boston vista, all Four Problems woven through the concluding tapestry.
images.short_caption: Full circle — the firm's lifecycle, synthesized.
images.internal_editorial_note: Final chapter image. Should feel conclusive but not closed — the Neon Edge endures.
images.crop_strategy_desktop: Full-width panoramic preserving the lifecycle timeline/tapestry
images.crop_strategy_mobile: Center on Zeeva; lifecycle panorama as ambient depth

images.visual_summary: A wide establishing shot of the New Boston 2077 cyberpunk cityscape. The image serves as the site-wide hero and landing-page backdrop — the world in which the entire narrative unfolds, before any chapter begins.
images.detected_setting: New Boston 2077 — wide aerial or street-level panorama of the cyberpunk city with neon signage, towering structures, atmospheric haze, and urban density
images.fit_assessment: New Boston 2077.png is the definitive landing-page hero. Its wide cityscape establishes the world before any doctrinal content begins, serving as the visual gateway to the Neon Edge.
reasons_for_fit: Wide establishing shot is the canonical landing-page composition
Sets the New Boston 2077 world for the entire site
No chapter-specific doctrine — purely world-building
images.focal_region_notes: City skyline as the full focal subject; no single character anchor
images.mobile_crop_notes: Center crop on the densest/most iconic portion of the skyline; preserve vertical structures
images.alt_text: The cyberpunk skyline of New Boston 2077 stretches across the horizon — neon towers, atmospheric haze, and urban density define the world of the Neon Edge.
images.short_caption: New Boston 2077 — where the story begins.
images.internal_editorial_note: Site-wide hero. Also usable as a parallax background, OG image, or print cover element. File is 2.4MB — good resolution for hero use.
images.crop_strategy_desktop: Full-bleed hero banner; no crop needed for wide displays
images.crop_strategy_mobile: Center crop on the skyline's densest section; allow edges to bleed
